name: implemented_methods_suite
title: Implemented Methods Benchmark Suite
description: Cross-track benchmark suite for the dynanets methods implemented so far. This keeps the synthetic growth-family, staged MLP, MNIST pruning, and MNIST routing families visible in one place without pretending that they are all directly comparable on a single leaderboard.

benchmarks.label: synthetic-growth-family
benchmarks.description: Two-spirals 10D / 5000-row benchmark for the first dynamic-growth paper batch.

results.name: edge-growth-spirals10
results.notes: adaptation=edge_growth

results.notes: adaptation=dynamic_nodes

results.notes: adaptation=nest

results.notes: adaptation=gradmax

results.name: net2wider-spirals10
results.notes: adaptation=net2wider

results.name: den-spirals10
results.notes: adaptation=den

benchmarks.label: synthetic-wave1
benchmarks.description: Wave 1 preview benchmark for AdaNet-style staged growth and Han-style pruning on the synthetic track.

protocol.name: track_a_wave1_preview
protocol.description: Wave 1 preview protocol for the synthetic track. Extends the growth-family comparison set with AdaNet-style staged candidate selection and the Han-style weights-and-connections pruning approximation.

benchmarks.label: mnist-pruning
benchmarks.description: MNIST pruning/compression benchmark covering structured, sparse, progressive, and workflow-based methods.

protocol.name: track_b_mnist_phase6_preview
protocol.description: MNIST Phase 6 preview protocol for CNN pruning methods with minibatch GPU training. Compares fixed and wide CNN baselines with structured pruning, runtime-cost-aware pruning, sparse masking, progressive filter pruning, training-time reconfiguration, Network Slimming, and MorphNet.

benchmarks.label: mnist-routing
benchmarks.description: MNIST routing benchmark covering dynamic slimmable execution, conditional computation, Channel Gating, and SkipNet.

protocol.name: track_b_mnist_phase7_preview
protocol.description: MNIST Phase 7 preview protocol for routed CNN methods. Compares a fixed CNN baseline, a wide CNN baseline, and the first routing-family approximations for dynamic slimmable execution, conditional computation, and the stronger routing-family approximations.
